_comment: WORKED-EXAMPLE TEMPLATE — copy this shape for a new flight plan, then replace nodes/agents. Validates against flight-plan.schema.json. Snapshot: a 6-phase Full plan at Phase 3 in progress. Shows every status (done/in_progress/known/assumed), each workshop as its OWN node, verbatim user_input bubbles, a companion that WRAPS the build phases, a worker side-node, AND the harness seams as first-class nodes whose commands are ALWAYS router invocations (/eng-harness-flow --event …, never child-skill names): backpressure (post-spec) on the spine between spec and plan; harness-boot (pre-implement) before a phase; harness-retro (phase-end / plan-complete) at the seams. Harness nodes are shown here because this example machine HAS the router installed; omit them all when the Layer-1 probe misses (no ~/.agents/skills/eng-harness-flow/SKILL.md and no ~/.claude/skills fallback). the-flow.md is generated FROM this file.

agents.note: wraps the build phases; reviews each commit live (fire-and-forget pings); supersedes /plan-7.
drafts package READMEs in parallel during the Docs phase.

nodes.label: Research
nodes.user_input: set up the monorepo: web app, cli, and mcp server, clean architecture, TDD-first
nodes.note: 8-subagent dossier; landed on pnpm+turborepo, commander, fake-adapter TDD.

nodes.label: Deep research x3
nodes.user_input: double-check the cli name and parser choice online
nodes.note: CLI name 'cg' is free; commander over yargs; turborepo caching.

nodes.label: Spec
nodes.user_input: make it clean-arch with fakes for every adapter; full TDD

nodes.label: Workshop 1 · clean-arch boundaries
nodes.user_input: where exactly do the layer boundaries sit?

nodes.label: Workshop 2 · fake/adapter strategy
nodes.user_input: one fake per adapter, or shared fakes?
nodes.note: one fake per port; shared test factory.

nodes.label: Backpressure Check (post-spec seam)
nodes.user_input: check what we can prove deterministically before planning
nodes.note: Post-spec harness seam (routed via /eng-harness-flow) — recommended between spec and architect. Certainty: Partial; recommended a Phase 0 to add a smoke route + dependency-cruiser rule. Advisory, never blocks.

nodes.label: Plan
nodes.note: Status READY, 6 phases locked -> future phase nodes flip assumed->known. Folded in the backpressure Phase 0.

nodes.label: Harness pre-flight (pre-implement seam)
nodes.note: Pre-implement harness seam — the router proves the system runs before phase code; verdict narrated verbatim from its envelope (healthy / SLOW / UNHEALTHY / UNAVAILABLE). /plan-6 auto-fires it. UNAVAILABLE is not an error.

nodes.label: Next.js + Clean Arch
nodes.note: building; tasks.md present, review not yet run.

nodes.label: fix loop?
nodes.note: only if Phase 3 review returns REQUEST_CHANGES.

nodes.label: Harness phase-end (seam)
nodes.note: Phase-end harness seam — the router owns what happens here (drain-vs-harvest is its call; the user may see a [s/t/p/e/d/a] prompt).

nodes.label: Harness plan-complete (seam)
nodes.note: Plan-complete harness seam — fired by /plan-8 after the merge; the router owns the long-horizon reflection.
